2603.25250.title: Activation Matters: Test-time Activated Negative Labels for OOD Detection with Vision-Language Models
2603.25250.paper_summary: Out-of-distribution (OOD) detection aims to identify samples that deviate from in-distribution (ID). One popular pipeline addresses this by introducing negative labels distant from ID classes and detecting OOD based on their distance to these labels. However, such labels may present poor activation on OOD samples, failing to capture the OOD characteristics. To address this, we propose \underline{T}est-time \underline{A}ctivated \underline{N}egative \underline{L}abels (TANL) by dynamically evaluating activation levels across the corpus dataset and mining candidate labels with high activation responses during the testing process. Specifically, TANL identifies high-confidence test images online and accumulates their assignment probabilities over the corpus to construct a label activation metric. Such a metric leverages historical test samples to adaptively align with the test distribution, enabling the selection of distribution-adaptive activated negative labels. By further exploring the activation information within the current testing batch, we introduce a more fine-grained, batch-adaptive variant. To fully utilize label activation knowledge, we propose an activation-aware score function that emphasizes negative labels with stronger activations, boosting performance and enhancing its robustness to the label number. Our TANL is training-free, test-efficient, and grounded in theoretical justification. Experiments on diverse backbones and wide task settings validate its effectiveness. Notably, on the large-scale ImageNet benchmark, TANL significantly reduces the FPR95 from 17.5\% to 9.8\%. Codes are available at \href{https://github.com/YBZh/OpenOOD-VLM}{YBZh/OpenOOD-VLM}.
2603.25250.paper_summary_zh: 超出分佈（OOD）檢測旨在識別偏離內部分佈（ID）的樣本。一個流行的流程通過引入與ID類別距離較遠的負標籤來解決這個問題，並根據這些標籤的距離來檢測OOD。然而，這些標籤在OOD樣本上的激活可能較差，無法捕捉到OOD的特徵。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了\underline{T}est-time \underline{A}ctivated \underline{N}egative \underline{L}abels（TANL），通過動態評估整個語料數據集的激活水平，並在測試過程中挖掘高激活響應的候選標籤。具體而言，TANL在線識別高置信度的測試圖像，並累積它們在語料中的分配概率，以構建標籤激活度量。這樣的度量利用歷史測試樣本自適應地與測試分佈對齊，從而選擇分佈自適應的激活負標籤。通過進一步探索當前測試批次中的激活信息，我們引入了一種更細緻的批次自適應變體。為了充分利用標籤激活知識，我們提出了一種激活感知的分數函數，強調激活較強的負標籤，提升性能並增強對標籤數量的穩健性。我們的TANL不需要訓練，測試效率高，並且有理論依據。對多種骨幹和廣泛任務設置的實驗驗證了它的有效性。值得注意的是，在大規模的ImageNet基準上，TANL將FPR95顯著降低從17.5\%到9.8\%。代碼可在\href{https://github.com/YBZh/OpenOOD-VLM}{YBZh/OpenOOD-VLM}獲得。
2603.25250.author: Yabin Zhang et.al.
2603.25250.authors: Yabin Zhang, Maya Varma, Yunhe Gao, Jean-Benoit Delbrouck, Jiaming Liu, Chong Wang, Curtis Langlotz
